leadText: Throat Chop (Japanese: じごくづき Hell Thrust) is a damage-dealing Dark-type move introduced in Generation VII. It was TR95 in Pokémon Sword and Shield, and is TM221 in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet. In Pokémon Sun and Moon, it was one of the signature moves of Incineroar, but other Pokémon could still inherit it via breeding.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] This move effect may be in need of research. Reason: Does Throat Chop fail when used on a Pokémon already affected by Throat Chop? Does it inflict damage when used on a Pokémon affected by Throat Chop? Using the sound-based moves: If a Pokémon already had a sound-based moved selected for use in the current turn but gets hit by Throat Chop first, will the move fail or will it use Struggle? If it is used and fails, will this spend any PP of the sound-based move? If a Pokémon affected by Throat Chop is ordered to use a non-sound-based move but attempts to use a sound-based move anyway because it is disobedient, will the move fail or will it use Struggle? If it is used and fails, will this spend any PP of the sound-based move? Can the affected Pokémon still use a sound-based move through Mirror Move, Metronome, or Instruct? If it attempts to use a sound-based move in this manner but it is unable to, will the move fail or will it use Struggle? If a confused, paralyzed, or infatuated Pokémon affected by Throat Chop attempts to use a sound-based move, does the Throat Chop failure takes precedence? (In particular, is it still possible to hit itself from confusion by attempting to use a sound-based move while affected by Throat Chop?) Is Throat Chop's effect transferable by Baton Pass? You can discuss this on the talk page. Throat Chop inflicts damage and prevents the target from using sound-based moves for two turns. Status Z-Moves are unaffected by this effect. If the target was causing an uproar , it ends early. When the effect of Throat Chop for a Pokémon begins or ends, it does not display a battle message. If the only moves that would be usable by an affected Pokémon are sound-based moves, or it was locked into using a sound-based move by a Choice item or Encore , it will use Struggle .

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA The user attacks the target's throat, and the resultant suffering prevents the target from using moves that emit sound for two turns. S V The user attacks the target's throat, and the resultant suffering prevents the target from using sound-based moves for two turns.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD RT DX It damages an enemy and gives them the Throat Chop condition.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] This move originated as a TCG attack . The Japanese name of this move, じごくづき Jigokuzuki (Hell Thrust), refers to a technique developed by the professional wrestler Abdullah the Butcher .
